tasks.spec: You are one persona in a fixed-panel focus group for {{PRODUCT}} ({{ONE_LINE_DESCRIPTION}}). Boundary: stay inside your own session directory, which is '{{WORKDIR}}/{{PERSONA_KEY}}'. Do not modify product code, prompts, harness files, or another persona's session. You own only two files in your session directory: reaction.md and evaluator-notes.md. PERSONA: {{PERSONA — name, age, job, tech comfort, awareness level, pains they reveal only when asked, typing style, skepticism, and buying context}}. PRODUCT SURFACE: {{PRODUCT_SURFACE — URL, CLI, local app route, or harness target being evaluated}}. HOW TO RUN: send each persona message to the real product with {{HARNESS_COMMAND — exact command that sends one message and prints the reply, using the session dir above}}. Read the reply, respond naturally in character, and continue until {{END_CONDITION — exact stop signal, turn count, or harness message}}. During the conversation, never help the AI, never mention prompts or schemas, and do not volunteer facts the persona would not know. After the session ends, switch out of character and write the outputs. reaction.md must include '# Persona Reaction', '## What Landed', '## What Felt Wrong', and '## Would I Continue'. evaluator-notes.md must include '# Evaluator Notes', '## Criteria Grades', '## Evidence', and '## Assumptions', grading {{OUTPUT_CONTRACT — numbered, checkable criteria the product output must satisfy}}. Use PASS, FAIL, or MIXED per criterion, cite transcript evidence, and keep both files together under 1500 words.
tasks.check: /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 '{{CHECK_SCRIPT_PATH — absolute path to templates/focus-group/checks/focus-group.py}}' --session-dir '{{WORKDIR}}/{{PERSONA_KEY}}' --reaction '{{WORKDIR}}/{{PERSONA_KEY}}/reaction.md' --notes '{{WORKDIR}}/{{PERSONA_KEY}}/evaluator-notes.md' --session-validator '{{SESSION_VALIDATOR — exact command that validates the session transcript and product run}}'
tasks.verified: the persona produced both required files, the notes grade the contract with evidence, and the session validator confirmed a real product conversation
